community_service.community_service_a_description: Volunteer tutor with Boulder Public Library STEM Afterschool Program, helping middle school students with basic coding (Scratch and Python), algebra, and introductory physics concepts. I typically work with a consistent group of 6–8 students from underrepresented backgrounds in STEM, helping them with homework, building confidence in math, and running simple hands-on science demonstrations (balloon rockets, paper circuit cards, etc.). I also help design and set up monthly themed workshops, like "Intro to Robotics" and "Women in Space Exploration," where we spotlight scientists and astronauts and connect STEM concepts to real-world careers.
community_service.community_service_b_description: Volunteer event coordinator and general helper for the Denver Urban Garden Network, focusing on weekend community garden workdays and seasonal food donation drives. I assist with organizing volunteers, maintaining sign-in sheets, coordinating tool sharing, and doing physical work like building raised beds, weeding, and planting. I also help manage the collection and delivery of surplus produce to a local food pantry, and I maintain simple spreadsheets tracking weekly yields and donations.
community_service.community_service_c_description: Peer mentor in the University of Colorado Boulder Engineering Honors Residential Academic Program. I provide informal mentoring and structured support for first-year engineering students, particularly women and first-generation college students. This includes leading biweekly study sessions, hosting time-management and exam-prep workshops, and maintaining an open-door policy in the residence hall for questions about course selection, research opportunities, and adjusting to college life. I also serve as a liaison between students and program staff when issues or concerns arise.

career_goals_and_background.career_goals_and_biographical_informatio:
I am a second-year undergraduate at the University of Colorado Boulder, majoring in Aerospace Engineering Sciences with a minor in Applied Mathematics. I grew up in Aurora, Colorado, in a family that encouraged curiosity but had limited exposure to STEM careers—my mom is a medical assistant and my dad drives for a regional delivery company. My fascination with space started early: my grandfather kept a worn copy of Heinlein’s "Have Space Suit—Will Travel" on his shelf, and I read it so many times the spine separated. That story, along with watching night launches from Cape Canaveral online, made space feel less like science fiction and more like a future I could help build.

Academically, I am focusing on guidance, navigation, and control (GNC) and small satellite design. I currently work as an undergraduate research assistant in a CubeSat lab, helping test attitude control algorithms using a tabletop air-bearing platform. My long-term goal is to work on autonomous navigation systems for deep-space probes and planetary landers, ideally at a place like JPL or a commercial space company contributing to lunar and Mars missions. I’m particularly interested in how we can make spacecraft more robust and adaptable, so that exploration is safer and more affordable.

As a first-generation college student, I’m also committed to mentoring younger students who don’t see themselves represented in aerospace. I volunteer as a STEM tutor and peer mentor because I know how much difference it makes to have someone say, "You belong here." Ultimately, I hope to contribute both technically—designing reliable systems that expand human presence in space—and socially, by helping open doors for the next generation of explorers.
